data.title: Surgery Partners Announces Idaho Falls Hospital Divestiture Agreement
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Surgery Partners ( (SGRY) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 21, 2026, Surgery Partners and Intermountain Health placed into escrow signed agreements under which Intermountain would acquire Surgery Partners’ ownership interests in Mountain View Hospital and Idaho Falls Community Hospital, valuing the Idaho Falls facilities at about $1.15 billion and providing approximately $795 million in consideration to Surgery Partners, subject to customary adjustments. The transaction, announced publicly on July 24, 2026, marks a major step in Surgery Partners’ portfolio optimization strategy, is contingent on physician approvals, regulatory clearances and other closing conditions, preserves existing physician ownership of Mountain View Hospital, and supports the company’s strategic shift toward ambulatory surgery centers while it reaffirms its 2026 revenue and Adjusted EBITDA outlook excluding the deal’s impact.

The sale involves hospital operations in Idaho Falls that include 126 beds, more than 150 physicians and a broad mix of surgical and acute care services, with Intermountain positioned as the new owner alongside longstanding physician partners. Company leadership framed the divestiture as simplifying ongoing operations, sharpening strategic focus and potentially enhancing long-term shareholder value, while warning that completion is not assured until all approvals are secured and noting that final net cash proceeds cannot yet be reasonably estimated.

The most recent analyst rating on (SGRY) stock is a Buy
with a $19.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Surgery Partners stock,
see the SGRY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SGRY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SGRY is a Neutral.

Overall score reflects solid cash generation and reiterated guidance, offset by high leverage and ongoing net losses. Technical signals are neutral-to-soft, and valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SGRY stock,
click here.

More about Surgery Partners

Surgery Partners, Inc., headquartered in Brentwood, Tennessee, is a healthcare services company focused on short-stay surgical care delivered through a differentiated outpatient model. Founded in 2004, it operates more than 200 locations in 30 U.S. states, including ambulatory surgery centers, surgical hospitals, multi-specialty physician practices and urgent care facilities, emphasizing high-quality, cost-effective surgical and ancillary services.

Sierra Bancorp, parent of Bank of the Sierra and one of the highest-rated U.S. community banks, focuses on serving retail and commercial customers in California’s South San Joaquin Valley and several Central Coast counties through a network of 34 branches and digital channels. The institution has built a long record of consistent shareholder returns, with regular cash dividends paid every year since 1987 and quarterly payouts since 1999.

The dividend, which marks the company’s 110th consecutive quarterly cash dividend, will be paid on August 10, 2026, to shareholders of record as of August 3, 2026, underscoring management’s confidence in the bank’s capital position and financial performance for the quarter ended June 30, 2026.

Tompkins Financial Corporation reported record earnings for the third consecutive quarter, with diluted earnings per share reaching $2.04 in the second quarter of 2026, a 36% increase from a year earlier and 12.1% higher than the first quarter. Net income for the quarter rose to $29.3 million, while first-half 2026 net income climbed 34.6% year over year to $55.4 million, driven by higher net interest income, stronger net interest margin and solid loan growth.

Net interest income grew 23% year over year in the second quarter to $74 million as net interest margin widened to 3.58%, supported by higher yields on earning assets and controlled funding costs, while average loans and deposits both expanded versus 2025. Despite a sharp drop in noninterest income following the sale of its insurance subsidiary in late 2025, Tompkins reduced noninterest expenses and maintained stable asset quality metrics, and its board on July 23, 2026 approved a 13% dividend increase, signaling confidence in the company’s capital strength and earnings momentum.

data.title: KULR Sells Bitcoin Holdings to Eliminate Credit Facility
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from KULR Technology Group ( (KULR) ).

During the period from July 9 to July 23, 2026, KULR Technology Group, Inc. sold approximately 333 bitcoin in open-market transactions at a weighted average price of about $64,538 per BTC, generating roughly $21.5 million in gross proceeds. The sales were part of the company’s treasury management strategy and were completed against a backdrop of heightened market volatility.

KULR used the net proceeds to fully repay its $20 million credit facility with Coinbase Credit, leaving no principal outstanding and positioning the company with a largely debt-free balance sheet. With all 565 BTC previously pledged as collateral expected to be returned and approximately 760 BTC still held as of July 23, 2026, the move is intended to cut interest expense, reduce collateral and liquidation risk, and increase financial flexibility under its newly appointed Board and CFO.

The transaction underscores KULR’s disciplined approach to capital allocation and risk management, balancing continued exposure to bitcoin’s potential upside with a stronger financial foundation. Management framed the decision as a proactive step to prioritize stockholder value while the company continues to scale its core battery technology operations.

Spark’s Take on KULR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KULR is a Neutral.

The score is held back most by weak financial performance—large ongoing losses and heavy negative free cash flow—despite solid revenue growth and low current leverage. Offsetting this, the earnings call points to improving commercial traction and a substantial gross-margin step-up with continued cost discipline, though execution risk around the planned H2 production ramp remains high. Technically, the stock shows a mixed picture with longer-term support above key moving averages but muted near-term momentum, while valuation lacks support given negative earnings and no dividend.

data.title: NewAmsterdam Pharma Wins EMA Backing for Obicetrapib Therapies
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

NewAmsterdam Pharma Company ( (NAMS) ) has provided an update.

On July 24, 2026, NewAmsterdam Pharma and its European partner Menarini Group reported that the European Medicines Agency’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use issued a positive opinion backing marketing authorization for Ubeslo, an obicetrapib monotherapy, and Evlarco, a fixed-dose combination of obicetrapib and ezetimibe, for patients with primary hypercholesterolemia and mixed dyslipidemia. The recommendation, which draws on positive Phase 3 BROADWAY, BROOKLYN and TANDEM trial results showing substantial LDL-C reductions with a placebo-like tolerability profile, moves obicetrapib a step closer to EU-wide approval, potentially unlocking significant royalty and milestone streams for NewAmsterdam while positioning Menarini to launch a novel oral lipid-lowering option across Europe pending a final European Commission decision expected in the second half of 2026.

Under their licensing deal, Menarini will lead regulatory and commercialization activities in Europe and NewAmsterdam will receive tiered double-digit royalties on regional sales plus up to €833 million linked to clinical, regulatory and commercial milestones, deepening the companies’ strategic alignment in cardiovascular disease. The advance of obicetrapib, including ongoing Phase 3 studies such as the PREVAIL cardiovascular outcomes trial, underscores the industry’s push to address persistent LDL-C goal shortfalls and residual cardiovascular risk despite widespread use of high-intensity statins and other lipid-lowering therapies, with investors likely to view the CHMP opinion as a key de-risking event for NewAmsterdam’s late-stage pipeline and its European market ambitions.

The most recent analyst rating on (NAMS) stock is a Buy
with a $58.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NewAmsterdam Pharma Company stock,
see the NAMS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NAMS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NAMS is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by heavy ongoing losses and significant cash burn despite a strong, low-debt balance sheet. Technicals are also weak with the stock trading below major moving averages. Valuation is difficult to support due to negative earnings and no dividend, while recent corporate actions modestly improve flexibility and alignment but do not offset core profitability and cash-flow risks.

To see Spark’s full report on NAMS stock,
click here.

More about NewAmsterdam Pharma Company

NewAmsterdam Pharma Company N.V. is a late-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing oral, non-statin therapies to lower low-density lipoprotein cholesterol in patients at risk of cardiovascular disease who do not adequately respond to or tolerate existing treatments. Its lead candidate, obicetrapib, is a once-daily, low-dose CETP inhibitor being evaluated as monotherapy and in a fixed-dose combination with ezetimibe, primarily as an adjunct to statin therapy in metabolic and cardiovascular disease populations.

The Menarini Group, which holds exclusive commercialization rights for obicetrapib in Europe, is a Florence-based international pharmaceutical and diagnostics company with a broad portfolio spanning cardiometabolic, oncology, gastroenterology and other major therapeutic areas, and reported $5.5 billion in consolidated turnover with operations in 140 countries and more than 17,000 employees.

Average Trading Volume: 1,308,720

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $3.71B

data.title: FangDD Receives Nasdaq Notice Over Sub-US$1 Share Price, Given 180 Days to Comply
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Fangdd Network Group ( (DUO) ) has issued an announcement.

Fangdd Network Group Ltd., a Shenzhen-based Chinese property technology company listed on Nasdaq as DUO, specializes in digitalizing real estate transactions through SaaS tools and data-driven platforms. Its solutions use mobile internet, cloud computing and artificial intelligence to help real estate participants streamline and modernize deal execution across China.

On July 24, 2026, FangDD disclosed that Nasdaq notified the company on July 22, 2026 that it is not in compliance with the exchange’s minimum bid price rule after its Class A shares closed below US$1 for 30 consecutive business days from June 8 to July 21, 2026. Nasdaq has granted FangDD an initial 180-day grace period to January 19, 2027 to restore its bid price to at least US$1 for ten straight trading days, and while the notice does not immediately affect the listing or trading of its shares, the company’s plan to monitor the stock and consider remedial options underscores continued pressure on its market valuation and the importance of maintaining exchange compliance for shareholders.

If FangDD fails to regain compliance within the first 180-day window, it may be eligible for a further 180-day extension, provided it meets other Nasdaq Capital Market listing requirements and formally commits to curing the deficiency. The announcement signals that, although there is no immediate delisting risk, investors face an extended period in which FangDD’s share price performance and any potential corporate actions aimed at boosting the bid price will be closely watched as part of its broader effort to protect its U.S. listing status.

Spark’s Take on DUO Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DUO is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing operating losses and sustained cash burn) and very bearish technical signals (price below all key moving averages with negative MACD and deeply oversold momentum). A low-debt balance sheet in recent years provides some risk offset, but valuation support is limited due to negative earnings and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on DUO stock,
click here.

More about Fangdd Network Group

Fangdd Network Group Ltd. is a China-based property technology company listed on Nasdaq under the ticker DUO. It provides digitalization services for real estate transactions, offering modular SaaS-powered products and solutions that leverage mobile internet, cloud computing, big data and artificial intelligence to modernize how market participants conduct property deals.

The company focuses on customer-oriented tools for brokers, agents and other stakeholders in the Chinese real estate market. By embedding advanced analytics and cloud-based platforms into transaction workflows, FangDD aims to streamline processes and enhance efficiency across the industry.

Its market positioning centers on being a leading proptech provider in China’s real estate sector, where digital transformation of transaction processes is increasingly important for competitiveness and compliance. FangDD’s technology suite is designed to support scalable, data-driven operations for real estate professionals nationwide.

Average Trading Volume: 261,273

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

data.title: Sadot Group Settles Debts Through Share Issuance Agreements
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest update is out from Sadot Group ( (SDOT) ).

On July 22, 2026, Sadot Group Inc. reached a Debt Settlement and Share Issuance Agreement with Rocket Capital NY LLC to resolve all disputes arising from a March 14, 2025 future receipts financing and related litigation in New York, agreeing to settle an asserted $599,582.62 obligation through an agreed debt amount of $500,000 satisfied by issuing 26,581 common shares and securing mutual releases and dismissal of the pending case. Rocket consented to daily share sale limits tied to 15% of Nasdaq trading volume to mitigate market impact, highlighting Sadot’s effort to reduce contested liabilities while managing potential dilution and trading pressure on its stock.

On July 23, 2026, Sadot also settled obligations under a promissory note held by former chief financial officer Jennifer Black, converting $466,617.73 of principal and default interest into 26,199 common shares and issuing a new unsecured Severance Note for $409,082.17 covering unpaid severance and compensation. The Severance Note carries a 10% annual interest rate, increasing after maturity, and is non-transferable and non-convertible, while Black accepted mutual releases and the same 15% daily trading cap on share sales, allowing the company to restructure past-due debts and executive claims into equity and a defined unsecured obligation.

Spark’s Take on SDOT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SDOT is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by weak financial performance (sharp TTM revenue decline, large losses, negative equity, and ongoing negative operating/free cash flow). Technicals are mixed with only modest near-term support (positive MACD, neutral RSI) but longer-term downtrend pressure (well below the 200-day average). Corporate actions provide some offset via strategic acquisitions and reduced legal/financing overhang, but Nasdaq equity compliance risk remains a notable overhang, and valuation is limited by losses and no indicated dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SDOT stock,
click here.

More about Sadot Group

Sadot Group Inc. is a publicly listed company whose common stock trades on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The company operates in sectors that expose it to structured financing arrangements and promissory note funding, reflecting an active use of capital markets and private credit to support its corporate activities and obligations.

Returns on average assets and equity improved to 2.04% and 20.34%, respectively, while net interest margin rose to 4.57%, supported by robust loan growth, deposit expansion and careful balance sheet management.

Total assets reached about $1.4 billion at June 30, 2026, as gross loans and total deposits each climbed to roughly $1.2 billion, reflecting double-digit quarterly growth and a 38.1% increase in deposits year over year. Credit quality remained solid, with a modest credit loss reversal, low net charge-offs and an allowance for credit losses at 0.91% of total loans, while capital ratios stayed comfortably above regulatory minimums despite rapid asset growth.

During the quarter, the company completed a leadership transition effective May 1, 2026, as long-time chairman Moishe Gubin assumed the additional role of chief executive officer, Braden R. Smith became president and former CEO Timothy Terry retired but continued to assist with succession. Management emphasized continued execution of strategic growth initiatives, including expansion into complementary services via OptimumFinance, LLC, which closed its first $14.2 million loan funded by a $14.0 million note, enhancing yield and diversifying revenue.

Noninterest income rose to $2.5 million in the second quarter of 2026 on higher gains from SBA loan sales and increased fee income, while noninterest expenses climbed to $8.4 million mainly due to higher compensation costs, keeping the efficiency ratio under 50%. Funding trends were favorable, with strong growth across all deposit categories and a reduction in Federal Home Loan Bank advances, underscoring improved balance sheet flexibility and positioning the bank for ongoing earnings momentum.

The company’s expanding profile was underscored by recent external recognition, including a number 49 ranking out of 3,465 U.S. community banks by S&P Global Market Intelligence in the first quarter of 2026 and a top-10% placement in Raymond James’s Community Bankers Cup in the second quarter. These accolades, alongside new analyst coverage, signal rising visibility among investors and reinforce OptimumBank’s positioning as a high-performing community bank focused on sustained growth and long-term shareholder returns.

OptimumBank Holdings, Inc. is a financial holding company based in Fort Lauderdale that owns 100% of OptimumBank, a Florida-chartered commercial bank, as well as non-bank subsidiaries OptimumHUD Loans and OptimumFinance. The group focuses on relationship-based community banking, commercial real estate lending, government-guaranteed SBA loans and complementary financing services, with a growing presence in deposit gathering and specialized lending across its markets.

On July 24, 2026, Dyadic International announced that Nasdaq confirmed the company has regained compliance with Listing Rules 5550(a)(2) and 5550(b), allowing its common stock to remain listed and traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market. data.title: Safety Insurance to be Acquired by MAPFRE U.S.A.
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Safety Insurance Group ( (SAFT) ) has shared an update.

On July 23, 2026, Safety Insurance Group, Inc. agreed to be acquired by MAPFRE U.S.A. Corp. in an all-cash merger under which a MAPFRE subsidiary will merge into Safety Insurance, which will become a wholly owned unit of MAPFRE. Each outstanding share will be converted into the right to receive $105 in cash, with restricted stock and performance share awards vesting and being cashed out, and directors and executives have signed voting agreements to support the deal.

The transaction, which is subject to shareholder approval, antitrust clearance, insurance regulatory approvals and other customary conditions, includes no-shop provisions, detailed termination rights and reciprocal break-up fees of about $46 million for Safety Insurance and $112 million for MAPFRE in specified scenarios. MAPFRE has secured committed equity financing from its Spanish parent to fund the purchase price and potential termination payments, and the merger agreement obliges Safety Insurance to operate in the ordinary course while limiting alternative deal pursuits, underscoring MAPFRE’s strategic push to deepen its U.S. insurance footprint and the potential for ownership and governance change for existing shareholders and other stakeholders.

Spark’s Take on SAFT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SAFT is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong underlying financial resilience (low leverage and consistently positive cash generation) and reinforced by a highly favorable M&A catalyst with a large cash premium. data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

HCA Healthcare ( (HCA) ) has provided an update.

HCA Healthcare reported its second-quarter 2026 results on July 24, 2026, posting an 8.7% year-on-year rise in revenue to $20.23 billion and a 2.8% increase in net income to $1.70 billion, with diluted EPS up 11.6% to $7.62 and adjusted EBITDA up 4.6% to $4.03 billion. Same-facility admissions rose 2.5% and ER visits 3.6%, while inpatient and outpatient surgeries declined, and the company also disclosed that first-half 2026 revenue reached $39.34 billion with net income of $3.32 billion.

The quarter was shaped by a negative payer mix shift from higher uninsured volumes tied to health insurance exchange coverage losses, which HCA estimated reduced pre-tax income by about $400 million, partially offset by roughly $400 million of incremental net benefit from Medicaid Supplemental Payment Programs, largely from Florida. Cash flow from operations fell sharply to $2.34 billion from $4.21 billion a year earlier, as HCA spent $1.23 billion on capital expenditures, repurchased 4.75 million shares for $2.06 billion, and maintained sizeable debt of $49.72 billion, while its board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.78 per share and the company trimmed full-year 2026 guidance to reflect greater exchange-related headwinds and higher expected Medicaid support.

The most recent analyst rating on (HCA) stock is a Hold
with a $369.00 price target.

The ceremony at Nasdaq MarketSite in Times Square underscores D-Wave’s bid to align with a leading technology exchange as it emphasizes its commercial traction and next phase of growth.
